Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Actions
Off Calendar Jul-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-300066 Estate of Willie Lee Hughes and PES-17-300905 Estate of Lauretta E.B. Hughes.

Appearances: Thomas Wrobel appeared by telephone for Lauretta Hughes.
Donna Quan and Christy Styer appeared by telephone for Sean Hughes.
Jessica Rowen-Perl appeared by telephone for Maichael Valencia.

The Court stated that it is not that concerned that the request for Court retention under CCP 664.6 was not identified in the petition, that the Parties can't agree on fees, and that statutory and extraordinary fees and costs have to be reviewed and approved by the Court. The Court also asked the Parties if they agree that their fee agreement is not binding on the Court. Ms. Quan stated that she has no problem with that and that it is her understanding that fees at the discretion of the Court. Ms. Perl stated that she is okay with it and that she does not object to the accounting as amended. Mr. Worth stated that he has no objection. The Court noted that it can't approve final distribution because they still have the issues of fees. Ms. Quan stated that the petition does ask for statutory fees, extraordinary fees and costs. The Court stated that the fee request from Sean seems a little high. Ms. Qaun stated that this was an eight-year administration, that the work involved tax issues, sale of property, and that as indicated in Mr. Bassi's declaration the statutory fees are inadequate to compensate for the amount of extraordinary work that was required for this administration. Ms. Quan also stated that they had to go back to the date of death to quantify the amount of rent that was calculated and that the accounting began at date of death, not with the date of appointment. The Court noted that the Petition for Preliminary Distribution, etc. is taken off calendar by Ms. Perl. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Actions, and the Amended Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Actions (subject to review of the orders). The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, Petition for Settlement and Allowance of Statutory and Extraordinary Fees, etc. is under submission.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution And For Order Surcharging Yvonne Mitchell Distribution Share
Off Calendar May-28-2019 Continued To Jul-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Bassi for Sean Hughes.
Jessica Perl for Michael Valencia.
Thomas Wrobel for Yvonne Mitchell.

The Court stated that given the state of things now where the real property is the only asset, it is not inclined to allow preliminary distribution. Ms. Perl stated that Cynthia Green just passed away, that she thinks there may be one assignment and that she was hoping they could hold out and then help Yvonne. Mr. Bassi stated that they are on their fourth revision of the accounting in Willie Hughes' estate, that he thinks it is close to being finalized, that once it's finalized he can file for final distribution and that there is an issue with rents, but he thinks it's close. Mr. Wrobel stated that he would like to work out the issues, but in order to do that they need distribution from Willie's estate. Mr. Bassi stated that he does not believe Yvonne has the financial wherewithal to purchase the property, that the house should be sold and suggested a 60-day continuance so that the parties will have a better idea as to where things stand. Mr. Wrobel stated that they have two assignments, that he has not filed them with the Court and that now that Cynthia has passed away, he is hoping to re-assign Cynthia's children. The Court questions whether Ms. Green's estate will need to be probated. The Petition for Preliminary Distribution is continued to July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to have met and conferred as to how they will proceed and whether there is a desire and ability to work on something.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order To Vacate Order Appointing Michael Valencia Administrator With Will Annexed [Ccp 473(B) And Revoking Letters And For Order Appointing Petitioner Executor Of The Will And To Appoint Executor
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Perl for Michael Valencia (Administrator).
Greer Smith for and with Yvonne Mitchell.
Michael Bassi and Donna Quan for Sean Hughes.

Mr. Greer addressed the issue of whether or not there was excusable neglect as to the Attorney. The Court expressed concern about the amount of time it took to raise this issue, stated that removal is not properly the Court and that she did not raise a proper basis under CCP 473(b). The Petition for Order to Vacate Order Appointing Michael Valencia, etc. is denied. Mr. Bassi or Ms. Quan are to submit a form of order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gino Molinari for Yvonne Mitchell (Petitioner).
Donna Quan for Sean Hughes (Objector).

Mr. Molinari stated that he has had great difficulty contacting Ms. Mitchell, that he can not speak on her behalf, and that he suggested to her to withdraw her petition, but he does not have authority to do that. Mr. Molinari argued the 30-day rule, stated that a petition was not filed for three years, that he spoke with opposing Counsel and said that he does not have a viable defense, and that it is discretionary upon the Court. Ms. Qaun stated that the papers propose a neutral who is ready and has a $750,000.00 bond, and that he is willing and able to be appointed and get the estate administered. The Court granted Mr. Hughes Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed for the appointment of Michael Valencia (subject to review of the order) with bond in the amount of $750,000.00. The Court stated that under Probate Code 8001, Ms. Mitchell waived any priority she may have had. Status on close of the Estate is set for January 15, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court will also grant the ex parte petition to waive notice to certain parties.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-05-2017 Continued To Jul-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gino Molinari for Yvonee Mitchell.
Donna Quan for Sean Hughes (Objector).
Lois Loretta Mitchell in Propria Persona.

Mr. Molinari requested a continuance stating that his Client is ill, that he is recommending they withdraw Ms. Mitchell's petition and that she would not give him authority over the phone to withdraw it. Ms. Quan requested appointment of her Client with will annexed, stated that her Client has a bond in the amount of $750,000.00, that the Estate has not been probated in four years and that she would like the Court to decide this matter today. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of Will are continued to July 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
